content:
- Updated the task markdown to completed with `<passes>true</passes>` and checked every acceptance box, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- No AGENTS.md update is needed: the only friction was local to this task (`README` word budget plus the strict README operator-workspace parser), and the new contract tests now encode both constraints.
- Next: stage everything including the plan/progress/task-switch artifacts, commit with validation evidence, push, and stop.

- After the first completion commit/push, Ralph regenerated `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` again, leaving the tree dirty with machine-generated summary churn.
- This does not look like concurrent user work; it is the same Ralph summary surface already touched by task switching.
- Next: commit those two regenerated summaries in a small follow-up commit, push again, confirm a clean tree, and stop.

- Read prior progress log and saw the previous task was completed and Ralph regenerated current task summaries.
- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md`; it currently lists one active candidate: `story-03-docs-api-contracts/task-10-docs-tls-configuration-reference.md` with `<passes>false</passes>`.
- Read `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`; all visible bug tasks are already `<passes>true</passes>`, so I am now checking the candidate task file itself for blockers/priority before selecting it.
- Next if unblocked: write that task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop immediately.

- Confirmed the actual stack from the codebase: Cargo workspace for `runner`/`setup-sql`, plus Go 1.26 verify-service code under `cockroachdb_molt/molt`; docs and OpenAPI live in repo root.
- Confirmed the only active task in `.ralph/current_tasks.md` is `.ralph/tasks/story-03-docs-api-contracts/task-10-docs-tls-configuration-reference.md` with `<passes>false</passes>`.
- Inspected the task file directly: it has no `<blocked_by>` tags and no bug tasks remain unblocked/failing in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, so this task is the highest-precedence selectable work item.
- Next: write that exact path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop immediately.

- Read prior progress log and confirmed the current task pointer already targets story-03 docs task 10.
- Loaded the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions from disk to follow the mandated workflow.
- Next: inspect the task file to determine whether this turn is planning-only (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).

- Confirmed the docs-task plan format from sibling tasks: task markdown gets a `<plan>` tag and the separate plan file ends with `Plan path:` plus an execution marker.
- Verified the key doc constraints: `README.md` is already 1249 words against a 1250-word contract, and there is no `docs/` directory yet.
- Chosen design for the next turn: create a dedicated `docs/tls-configuration.md`, keep README quick starts short with links, and use a dedicated docs contract test/helper as the single owner of the TLS reference surface.

- Created the missing plan artifact for task 10 and added its `<plan>` tag to the task markdown.
- Chose a dedicated `docs/tls-configuration.md` design because the README is already at 1249/1250 words; the plan marks `NOW EXECUTE` for the next turn.
- Next: execute the plan via vertical TDD slices, starting with a failing docs contract for the new TLS reference file and README link, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` only.

- Read prior progress log, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill files from disk.
- Confirmed the current task is story-03 docs task 10; previous turn already created a plan and expected this turn to execute it.
- Next: inspect the task markdown and linked plan, determine whether it is still `NOW EXECUTE`, then either execute via vertical TDD or revert to planning if the design is wrong.

- Read the task markdown and linked plan file; the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must implement it rather than create a new plan.
- Confirmed the repo still has no `docs/` directory and README currently holds the scattered TLS guidance the plan intends to consolidate.
- Next: inspect existing README contract tests/helpers, add the first failing TLS reference contract slice, then implement the minimal doc and README link to turn it green.

- Added the first dedicated TLS reference contract test, ran it red on the missing doc, then turned it green by creating `docs/tls-configuration.md` and adding a README pointer.
- The first slice now passes through the public docs surface; next is to tighten the contract around the component mapping table, mode explanations, and forbidden-content exclusions.
- Before that expansion, I am checking the existing runner and verify config tests so the new doc only documents supported public fields and terminology.

- Completed the dedicated TLS reference doc through vertical slices: tracer bullet, mapping/modes, runner scenarios, then verify scenarios/OpenAPI cross-reference.
- Cleaned the README boundary by removing the duplicated runner-vs-verify TLS matrix, replacing it with links to `docs/tls-configuration.md`, and updated the README contract accordingly; focused runner tests are green.
- Next: run the required validation lanes `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; only after all pass will I update the task file, switch tasks, and commit/push.
